_fixture_meta.label: positive_static_permanent_delegate
_fixture_meta.notes: SYNTHETIC fixture — constructed from documented Token-2022 PermanentDelegate attack pattern (cryptorank.io/news, March 2026). The PermanentDelegate extension grants a designated authority unconditional power to transfer or burn any holder's tokens without their signature. Token 'RED' was cited as one example where tokens were burned 7 seconds after purchase. No stable mint address because these tokens are ephemeral. Developer should replace with a real captured fixture once the detector corpus accumulates these. Pattern: (1) Token-2022 program, (2) permanentDelegate = deployer address (non-null), (3) no freeze authority, (4) transfer fee = 0 bps (permanent delegate is the attack vector, not fee), (5) zero successful sell txs because the delegate burns tokens before they can be sold. Expected signal: permanent_delegate_active fires at weight 0.20 contribution. buy_sell_ratio fires at 999 (zero sells). Combined: moderate-low confidence since no sell tax. If simulation is run, the simulate will show token balance dropped to zero after buy (burned by delegate) — simulation_fail=true if sell is attempted after balance=0.

risks.name: Permanent Delegate
risks.description: Token has a permanent delegate that can transfer or burn any holder's tokens without their authorization.
